breaking.text: Broadcom beats Q1 estimates with record $19.3B revenue; AI chip revenue doubles to $8.4B
Oil surges 8%+ this week after Iranian IRGC Navy strikes American oil tanker in Persian Gulf
February jobs report due at 8:30 AM ET — economists expect 50K payrolls amid Kaiser Permanente strike drag

summary: The S&P 500 closed near 6,820 on Thursday, down 0.56%, decisively failing the 6,900 resistance level and erasing all 2026 gains as a triple threat of sticky inflation, hawkish Fed policy, and Middle East escalation rattled investors. Oil prices surged over 8% after Iran's Revolutionary Guard Navy struck an American oil tanker in the Persian Gulf, sending crude above $79 and triggering the commodity's biggest weekly gain since 2022. After the bell, Broadcom delivered a blowout Q1 with record revenue of $19.3 billion and AI semiconductor sales doubling year-over-year to $8.4 billion, sending shares up 5% in extended trading. Markets now brace for Friday's February jobs report, where economists expect a modest 50,000 payroll gain weighed down by a Kaiser Permanente strike that affected 31,000 workers during the BLS survey week.

highlights.text: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says $30B OpenAI investment 'might be the last time' before startup's potential IPO later this year
February nonfarm payrolls expected at just 50K as Kaiser Permanente strike affecting 31K workers weighs on healthcare hiring
American Airlines downgraded to neutral by Rothschild as surging jet fuel costs push EPS forecasts negative for 2026
Iran's IRGC Navy strikes American oil tanker near Strait of Hormuz, sending crude oil above $79 and triggering biggest weekly gain since 2022
Broadcom Q1 revenue hits record $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI chip revenue doubles to $8.4B; CEO sees path to $100B AI chip revenue by 2027
S&P 500 fails 6,900 resistance and turns negative for 2026, closing at 6,820 with VIX surging to 22.08 — highest since autumn 2025
